steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure on each side of the blank
steps.content: The blank separates two noun phrases: “the most prestigious award for a person of letters” (a noun phrase with modifiers) and “the Nobel Prize in Literature” (a noun phrase that renames or specifies the first). The second noun phrase is an appositive.

steps.title: Apply the appositive punctuation rule
steps.content:
Choice D correctly places the colon after the noun phrase to introduce its appositive, “the Nobel Prize in Literature.” The semicolons in A and B cannot connect a noun phrase to another noun phrase (semicolons join independent clauses only), and C places the colon before a modifier rather than before the appositive elaboration.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
